zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda FitzAlan於1264年出生在英國素可阿盧德爾時，她的父親Sir John FitzAlan III18歲，母親Isabel Mortimer16歲。她於1290年之前與Phillip Burnell在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1325年11月17日享年61歲。

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda FitzAlan was born in 1264, in Arundel, Sussex, England, her father, Sir John FitzAlan III, was 18 and her mother, Isabel Mortimer, was 16. She married Phillip Burnell before 1290, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 17 November 1325, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda FitzAlan est née en 1264, à Arundel, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Sir John FitzAlan III, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Isabel Mortimer, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé Phillip Burnell avant 1290, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 17 novembre 1325, à l’âge de 61 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Matilda FitzAlan(이)가 1264년 영국 수세스 아런들에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John FitzAlan III(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabel Mortimer(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 1290년 이전 스컷랜드에서 Phillip Burnell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1325년 11월 17일 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda FitzAlan родилась в 1264 г. в местности арундель, суссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John FitzAlan III было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Mortimer, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Phillip Burnell перед 1290 г. в местности Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 17 ноября 1325 г. в возрасте 61 год.
